reference: Thirteen-year-old female, with a mild history of exercise-induced asthma, presented twice in seven days with seizure activity. Her eyes were rolling back in her head, she was coughing, had forced muscle stiffness, loss of consciousness, crushing chest pain, elevated blood pressure, elevated heart rate, dilated pupils, blurred vision, and an inability to speak. The episodes lasted twenty to twenty-five minutes. There is no previous history of other illnesses. Could it possibly be epilepsy? Or a pulmonary embolism?
text: Thirteen-year-old female with a mild history of exercise-induced asthma presented twice in seven days with seizure activity. Her eyes were rolling back in her head. She was coughing, had forced muscle stiffness, loss of consciousness, crushing chest pain, elevated blood pressure, elevated heart rate, dilated pupils, blurred vision, and an inability to speak. The episodes lasted twenty to twenty-five minutes. There is no previous history of other illnesses. Could it possibly be epilepsy, or a pulmonary embolism?
